patches.find: After reviewing each claim against published data and the model's own equations, none produces a prediction that both derives from dome geometry and differs from standard physics. This review does not assume globe geometry is correct — the strongest arguments (Parts 2 and 2b) use only the dome's own stated parameters and equations to show internal contradictions that require no external frame of reference. Many referenced observations are real — the verdicts address interpretation, not the data. What would change this?
patches.replace: None produces a prediction that both derives from dome geometry and differs from standard physics. What would change this?
patches.rationale: Tighten the '0 Survive Scrutiny' scorecard sublabel from 81 words to 24 words. Retain the key discriminating criterion (derives from dome geometry + differs from standard physics) and the falsifiability link. Remove self-test framing and observation caveats — these belong in the evidence-roadmap or body text, not in a 3-second scorecard.
